headline: Oil extends gains, stocks sink as peace talk hopes fade
summary: Oil prices extended gains Friday and stocks struggled as investors worried about a lack of progress in ending the Middle East crisis, with Tehran keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed and the US maintaining a blockade of Iranian ports."So far, no meaningful progress has been made in negotiations, with both sides maintaining relatively firm positions, leaving the short-term outlook unclear.

content.title: Oil extends gains, stocks sink as peace talk hopes fade
content.summary: Oil prices extended gains Friday and stocks struggled as investors worried about a lack of progress in ending the Middle East crisis, with Tehran keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed and the US maintaining a blockade of Iranian ports."So far, no meaningful progress has been made in negotiations, with both sides maintaining relatively firm positions, leaving the short-term outlook unclear.

thumbnail.caption: Iran said it would not join talks to reopen the Strait of Hormuz while the United States maintained a blockade of the country's ports (-)

headline: Markets Recover With Focus on Big Tech Earnings Over Iran. Stock Futures Rise.
summary: Stocks looked set to rise on Friday as solid tech earnings helped reassure investors who were questioning whether the market could keep its recent rally going.  The three major indexes all fell on Thursday as tensions flared up in the Strait of Hormuz and International Business Machines and ServiceNow both reported unspectacular earnings, sparking another selloff in software stocks.  Oil prices were barely moving on Friday as President Donald Trump said the cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon would extend another three weeks.

content.title: Markets Recover With Focus on Big Tech Earnings Over Iran. Stock Futures Rise.
content.summary: Stocks looked set to rise on Friday as solid tech earnings helped reassure investors who were questioning whether the market could keep its recent rally going.  The three major indexes all fell on Thursday as tensions flared up in the Strait of Hormuz and International Business Machines and ServiceNow both reported unspectacular earnings, sparking another selloff in software stocks.  Oil prices were barely moving on Friday as President Donald Trump said the cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon would extend another three weeks.

headline: Meta axes 8,000 jobs to fund AI spending spree, Microsoft to follow suit

headline: Microsoft and Meta to cut thousands of jobs
summary: Microsoft and Meta, the company behind Instagram and WhatsApp, are planning to make significant cuts to their respective workforces as spending on artificial intelligence (AI) finds a new high gear.  Meta said it was laying off about 8,000 workers, or about 10% of its workforce, in a bid to make efficiency savings as it ramps up investment.  Meta had previously told investors that spending would top $160bn during 2026 - up from just shy of $120bn last year.

content.title: Microsoft and Meta to cut thousands of jobs

Meta had previously told investors that spending would top $160bn during 2026 - up from just shy of $120bn last year.

headline: Oil extends gains, stocks drop as peace talk hopes fade
summary:
Oil prices extended gains Friday and stock markets largely declined as investors worried about a lack of progress in ending the Middle East crisis, with Tehran keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed and the US maintaining a blockade of Iranian ports.However on Friday, "stocks are down across the board, risk sentiment is off and oil prices are higher", noted Neil Wilson, investor strategist at Saxo UK.
"There's no real progress in ending the war between the US and Iran."

content.title: Oil extends gains, stocks drop as peace talk hopes fade
content.summary:
Oil prices extended gains Friday and stock markets largely declined as investors worried about a lack of progress in ending the Middle East crisis, with Tehran keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed and the US maintaining a blockade of Iranian ports.However on Friday, "stocks are down across the board, risk sentiment is off and oil prices are higher", noted Neil Wilson, investor strategist at Saxo UK.
"There's no real progress in ending the war between the US and Iran."

thumbnail.caption: Iran said it would not join talks to reopen the Strait of Hormuz while the United States maintained a blockade of the country's ports (-)

headline: What Microsoft buyouts, Meta layoffs signal about Big Tech in AI age
summary: Earlier this week, Meta Platforms (META) announced plans for layoffs to come while Microsoft (MSFT) began offering voluntary buyout package to its employees. This all comes as both companies continue to raise their spending on AI. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland weigh in on the state of Big Tech as more of these layoffs become more common.

content.title: What Microsoft buyouts, Meta layoffs signal about Big Tech in AI age
content.description:
Earlier this week, Meta Platforms (META) announced plans for layoffs to come while Microsoft (MSFT) began offering voluntary buyout package to its employees. This all comes as both companies continue to raise their spending on AI.

Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland weigh in on the state of Big Tech as more of these layoffs become more common.

headline: Amazon Stock Eyes Breakout On Meta AI Chip Deal
summary: Amazon stock climbed early Friday toward a potential breakout following news it will provide its Graviton processors to Facebook parent Meta Platforms. Meta stock gained as well. The companies announced that Meta will use "tens of millions" of Amazon Web Services' Graviton CPUs (central processing units) to power its AI offerings, according to the announcement.

summary: SEATTLE, April 24, 2026--Meta has signed an agreement to deploy AWS Graviton processors at scale. The deal marks a significant expansion of a long-standing partnership between the two companies as Meta builds its next generation of AI.

content.summary: SEATTLE, April 24, 2026--Meta has signed an agreement to deploy AWS Graviton processors at scale. The deal marks a significant expansion of a long-standing partnership between the two companies as Meta builds its next generation of AI.

headline: Meta strikes deal with Amazon's cloud unit to use its CPU chips
summary: Meta Platforms and Amazon.com on Friday said Meta will use Amazon ‌Web Services' (AWS) Graviton5 central processing unit (CPU) chips, a ‌deal an AWS executive told Reuters would span multiple years and be worth ​billions of dollars. Meta will use "tens of millions of cores" worth of Graviton chips. Each chip itself contains 192 cores, but they can each be assigned to different tasks.

content.title: Meta strikes deal with Amazon's cloud unit to use its CPU chips
content.summary: Meta Platforms and Amazon.com on Friday said Meta will use Amazon ‌Web Services' (AWS) Graviton5 central processing unit (CPU) chips, a ‌deal an AWS executive told Reuters would span multiple years and be worth ​billions of dollars. Meta will use "tens of millions of cores" worth of Graviton chips. Each chip itself contains 192 cores, but they can each be assigned to different tasks.

headline: Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, Salesforce, and Stripe Join the Universal Commerce Protocol Tech Council
summary: Internet, Everywhere--(Newsfile Corp. - April 24, 2026) - Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, Salesforce, and Stripe are now a part of the Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP) Tech Council, the technical body that steers the open standard for agentic commerce - how AI agents work with businesses. They will join founding Tech Council members Google, Shopify, Etsy, Target, and Wayfair.As AI agents become a common interface for discovering and buying products, it's critical to have a shared, open protocol that..

headline: Is IBM’s Q1 Beat A Classic Valuation Trap?
summary: IBM (NYSE: IBM) reported Q1 2026 revenue of $15.9 billion, a 9% increase y-o-y, and Non-GAAP EPS of $1.91. This top-line success obscures a structural divergence: cyclical hardware strength is currently subsidizing a notable slowdown in high-margin Consulting and Software divisions.

content.summary: IBM (NYSE: IBM) reported Q1 2026 revenue of $15.9 billion, a 9% increase y-o-y, and Non-GAAP EPS of $1.91. This top-line success obscures a structural divergence: cyclical hardware strength is currently subsidizing a notable slowdown in high-margin Consulting and Software divisions.

headline: Amazon Expands AI Chip Reach And Deepens Healthcare Push With GLP‑1 Program

The agreement expands the reach of Amazon's in house chip ecosystem across AI and cloud infrastructure. Separately, Amazon launched a nationwide GLP-1 weight loss drug program through One Medical and Amazon Pharmacy. The new healthcare offering integrates telehealth, prescriptions, and fulfillment for obesity treatments in the US. At a share price of $255.08,...

headline: China to curb US investment in tech companies, Bloomberg News reports
summary: China plans to restrict top technology firms, including leading AI startups, from accepting U.S. capital without government approval, Bloomberg ‌News reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.  Chinese regulators, ‌including the National Development and Reform Commission, have recently instructed several private technology firms to reject ​U.S. investment in funding rounds unless explicitly approved, the report said.  Regulators have also decided on similar restrictions for TikTok owner ByteDance and do not ‌want the company to ⁠approve secondary share sales to U.S. investors without government approval, it added.

content.title: China to curb US investment in tech companies, Bloomberg News reports
content.summary: China plans to restrict top technology firms, including leading AI startups, from accepting U.S. capital without government approval, Bloomberg ‌News reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.  Chinese regulators, ‌including the National Development and Reform Commission, have recently instructed several private technology firms to reject ​U.S. investment in funding rounds unless explicitly approved, the report said.  Regulators have also decided on similar restrictions for TikTok owner ByteDance and do not ‌want the company to ⁠approve secondary share sales to U.S. investors without government approval, it added.

headline: Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman, Mag 7 earnings, Fed meeting: What to Watch
summary: Asking for a Trend Host Josh Lipton previews several of the biggest stories to come next week, including the start of the trial between OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) co-founders Sam Altman and Elon Musk; earnings results from Magnificent Seven tech companies Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Meta Platforms (META), and Apple (AAPL); and the Federal Reserve's latest interest rate decision to come at its April FOMC meeting.
